title: /design:audit metadata omits the packet's procedure-card surface
claim: /design:audit command metadata does not reflect the packet's actual procedure-card surface even though the registry and packet contract make design-audit/procedures part of audit routing.
counterevidenceSought: Searched command-metadata.json for design-audit/procedures, procedures/accessibility_audit, procedures/ai_slop_check, shared/procedures, polish_gate_orchestration, and procedures/; no matches were found.
alternativeExplanation: The schema may intentionally rely on SKILL.md to describe private procedure cards, but the current command projection names references/assets while omitting the declared procedure-card routing surface.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if command metadata schema is documented to omit private procedure cards for all modes, or if /design:audit metadata adds the audit procedure cards and shared polish card.

title: package_skill.py --check warns that design-audit/SKILL.md exceeds the recommended size budget
evidenceRefs: package_skill.py output: SKILL.md has 3281 words recommended max 3000
exactRule: SKILL.md recommended max: 3000 words; observed: 3281 words.
recommendation: Move non-routing explanatory detail into packet references while preserving runtime-critical routing and procedure selection.
